rules.title: Semantic divider must use <hr> or role='separator'
rules.description: When the divider represents a meaningful content boundary, it must use either a semantic <hr> element or an element with role='separator'. Using a plain <div> or <span> without role='separator' removes it from the accessibility tree.
rules.remediation: Replace <div class='divider'> with <hr class='divider'> for semantic use. For non-semantic contexts, add role='separator' to the element.

rules.title: Decorative divider must have role='none' and aria-hidden='true'
rules.description: When the divider is purely decorative (no thematic break meaning), it must have role='none' aria-hidden='true' to suppress AT announcement. Repeating decorative dividers without aria-hidden create noise in screen reader navigation.
rules.remediation: Add role='none' aria-hidden='true' to <hr class='divider'> when the divider is purely decorative.

rules.title: Divider must not be focusable
rules.description: The divider is not an interactive element. Adding tabindex to a divider makes it focusable, which creates a keyboard navigation trap and announces a non-interactive element to screen reader users.
rules.remediation: Remove any tabindex attribute from .divider elements.

rules.title: Color must not be the sole means of conveying information
rules.description: The divider color variants (neutral, primary, alpha-white, white) must not be used as the only way to communicate meaning or state. Color-only information violates WCAG 1.4.1.
rules.check: Color variant usage does not convey meaning without an additional non-color indicator
rules.remediation: Pair color variants with structural context, labels, headings, or other non-color indicators when the color is intended to convey meaning.

rules.title: No hardcoded visual values in component CSS
rules.description: Component CSS must not use hardcoded hex, rgba, or pixel values outside of token fallbacks in var().
rules.check: No raw color or size values outside var(--divider-*, fallback) patterns
rules.remediation: Replace hardcoded values with component-level tokens (--divider-*). Fallbacks inside var() are acceptable pending token.css update.

rules.title: Vertical divider must have aria-orientation='vertical'
rules.description: The separator role has aria-orientation='horizontal' by default. Vertical dividers must explicitly declare aria-orientation='vertical' for correct AT announcement.
rules.remediation: Add aria-orientation='vertical' to any <hr> or role='separator' element that uses .divider--vertical.

rules.title: Divider must not contain child elements
rules.description: The .divider element must have no children. Adding child elements to a self-contained divider breaks the component contract and may cause rendering issues.
rules.remediation: Remove any child elements from .divider. The component is self-contained.

rules.title: Component tokens should be defined in token.css
rules.description: The --divider-* token definitions should be formally defined in token.css under a Divider tokens section. Currently only active as inline var() fallbacks.
rules.remediation: Add a '/* Divider tokens */' section to token.css with the tokens listed in analysis.md Missing Tokens.

rules.title: Alpha-white and white variants should be used on dark backgrounds only
rules.description: The --divider-alpha-white and --divider-white modifiers use white/near-white colors that will be invisible on white backgrounds.
rules.check: .divider--alpha-white or .divider--white used only on dark or colored background contexts
rules.remediation: Ensure .divider--alpha-white and .divider--white are only placed on dark or colored backgrounds. Add a background color context comment in the implementation.
